briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby Housing Authority's 2026-2030 Strategic Plan
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider approving the Burnaby Housing Authority (BHA) Corporation’s strategic plan for 2026-2030. The BHA provides affordable housing in Burnaby. The plan will guide the BHA's work over the next four years.
briefs.source_body: 7.1 BHA - 2026-2030 STRATEGIC PLAN 1. BHA - 2026-2030 Strategic Plan 2026-03-24.pdf 2. Attachment 1 - BHA 2026-2030 Strategic Plan 2026-03-24_.pdf Purpose: To seek Council approval of the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation’s (BHA) 2026-2030 Strategic Plan. BHA - 2026-2030 Strategic Plan 2026-03-24.pdf Attachment 1 - BHA 2026-2030 Strategic Plan 2026-03-24_.pdf

briefs.title: Council to Vote on UBCM Membership Dues Payment for 2026
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider paying membership dues to the Union of British Columbia Municipalities (UBCM) for 2026. UBCM is an organization that represents local governments in BC. Council must approve the payment.
briefs.source_body: 7.2 UBCM 2026 MEMBERSHIP DUES 1. UBCM 2026 Membership Dues 2026-03-24.pdf 2. Attachment 1 - UBCM 2026 Membership Invoice 2026-03-24.pdf 3. Attachment 2 - Letter from the UBCM President 2026-03-24.pdf Purpose: To seek Council approval for payment of the Union of British Columbia Municipalities (UBCM) 2026 membership dues. UBCM 2026 Membership Dues 2026-03-24.pdf Attachment 1 - UBCM 2026 Membership Invoice 2026-03-24.pdf Attachment 2 - Letter from the UBCM President 2026-03-24.pdf

briefs.title: Council to Consider Water Metering Technology Contract Award
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider awarding a contract for supplying and delivering water metering technology. This contract supports the city's Universal Water Metering Strategy. Council is set to vote on approving this contract at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.action_required: Monitor Council's decision, as this may affect future water service costs.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant
briefs.source_body: 7.3 CA - SUPPLY AND DELIVERY WATER METERING TECHNOLOGY 1. CA - Supply and Delivery Water Metering Technology 2026-03-24.pdf 2. CA - Supply and Delivery Water Metering Technology 2026-03-24.pdf Attachment 1 - Universal Water Metering Stragtegy (Updated) (Council 2024-12-02)

briefs.title: Council to Consider Contract for Modular Housing Four-Plex Development
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider awarding a contract for a pilot project. The project involves building a modular four-plex housing unit at 8315 14th Avenue. Council's decision would allow the project to move forward.
briefs.source_body: 7.4 CA - DEVELOPMENT OF MODULAR HOUSING FOUR-PLEX UNIT AT 8315 14TH AVENUE 1. CA - Development Modular Housing Four-Plex 8315 14th Ave 2026-03-24.pdf Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the development of a modular four-plex housing unit as a pilot project. CA - Development Modular Housing Four-Plex 8315 14th Ave 2026-03-24.pdf

briefs.title: Council to Vote: Hired Equipment Services Contracts (19 Awards)
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider approving 19 separate contracts for Hired Equipment Services. These contracts would allow the city to rent equipment as needed for various projects. Council is set to vote on whether to approve these agreements.
briefs.source_body: 7.5 CA - HIRED EQUIPMENT SERVICES 1. CA - HE Services - All Contractors - 2026-03-24_.pdf Purpose: To obtain Council approval to award nineteen separate contracts (CA) for Hired Equipment Services. CA - HE Services - All Contractors - 2026-03-24_.pdf

briefs.title: Council to Vote: Asphalt Paving Contract Renewal
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider renewing a contract for asphalt paving services for the Engineering Department. Council must approve this contract renewal. The contract provides the city with asphalt paving for road maintenance and construction projects.
briefs.action_required: Monitor Council's decision if you are an asphalt paving contractor.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Amended High-Density Development at Carrigan Court
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider a proposal for an amended high-density mixed-use development at 3856 and 3870 Carrigan Court. The plan includes commercial, residential strata (individually owned units), and rental units. Council's decision would allow city staff to work with the applicant to create a detailed development plan for future consideration.
briefs.action_required: Stay informed about future council meetings regarding this development.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
briefs.source_body: 8.1 PDC - AMENDED DEVELOPMENT PROPOSAL ENQUIRY FOR 3856 AND 3870 CARRIGAN COURT - PROPOSED AMENDED HIGH-DENSITY MIXED-USE COMMERCIAL/RESIDENTIAL STRATA AND RENTAL DEVELOPMENT 1. briefs.title: Council to Consider: Amended Mixed-Use Development at 3900 Grand Promenade
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider forwarding Rezoning Application #24-23 to a future meeting for First and Second Reading. The application concerns an amended plan for a mixed-use commercial and residential development at 3900 Grand Promenade, as well as 9850 Austin Rd, and 9858 and 9898 Gatineau Place.

briefs.title: Proposed: Twelve-Storey Mixed-Use Development at 6535 Kingsway Up for Approval
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider forwarding a rezoning application for 6535 Kingsway to a future meeting for First and Second Reading. The application proposes a twelve-storey mixed-use development. This means Council is set to vote on whether to allow the project to move forward for further consideration.
briefs.action_required: Monitor City Council's future meetings for updates on this rezoning application.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Council to Consider Rezoning for Five-Storey Self-Storage on Norland Avenue
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider a rezoning application for 2757 Norland Avenue. The proposal is to build a five-storey self-storage facility. Council is set to vote on forwarding the application to First and Second Reading at a future meeting. This is the first step in the rezoning process. Rezoning means changing the permitted uses of a property.
briefs.action_required: Stay informed about Council's decision regarding this rezoning application.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby Poverty Reduction Strategy Endorsement
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider endorsing the Burnaby Poverty Reduction Strategy. The strategy aims to reduce poverty in Burnaby. It includes actions and goals to address poverty-related issues in the city.
briefs.action_required: Review the Burnaby Poverty Reduction Strategy to understand its potential impact.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Building Permit Activity Reports for January and February 2026
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will look at reports about building permits issued in January and February 2026. These reports give Council information about construction happening in the city during that time. The reports include details on the number and types of building permits issued.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: 2025 City Investment Report and 2026 Forecast
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will review the 2025 Investment Program's year-end report and consider the investment forecast for 2026. The report includes proposed investment limits for 2026. This could affect how the City manages its money.
briefs.action_required: Review the investment report to understand potential impacts on City finances.

briefs.title: Burnaby Council to Receive Update on Civic Projects in March 2026
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will get an update on major city projects in March 2026. This is for information only. Council will review the progress of projects like new community centres, parks, and infrastructure upgrades. No decisions will be made at this time.
briefs.source_body: 9.3 FMC - SU: SIGNIFICANT CIVIC PROJECTS - MARCH 2026 1. FMC - SU Significant Civic Projects – March 2026 2026-03-24.pdf Purpose: To provide Council with a status update (SU) on significant civic projects. FMC - SU Significant Civic Projects – March 2026 2026-03-24.pdf

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby 2050 Official Community Plan Implementation
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider a plan to put the Official Community Plan (OCP) into action. The OCP is a document that guides how the city will grow and change in the future. The plan includes steps and timelines for making the OCP's goals a reality.
briefs.action_required: Stay informed about the plan's progress and any opportunities for public input.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant
briefs.source_body: 9.4 PDC - OCP: BURNABY 2050 IMPLEMENTATION ACTION PLAN PROJECT INITIATION 1. PDC - OCP - Burnaby 2050 Implementation Action Plan 2026-03-24.pdf Purpose: To provide Council with an overview of the Official Community Plan (OCP) Implementation Action Plan. PDC - OCP - Burnaby 2050 Implementation Action Plan 2026-03-24.pdf

briefs.title: Proposed: Zoning Bylaw Amendment for 20-Storey Mixed-Use Building on Imperial Street
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider a zoning change for 5058 Imperial Street. A proposed bylaw would allow a 20-storey building with market rental apartments and ground-level commercial space. This new plan would replace a previously approved plan for a six-storey rental building. The first storey would be double-height to accommodate commercial businesses.
briefs.action_required: Monitor the Council decision if you operate a business near 5058 Imperial Street.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
briefs.source_body: 11.1.1 #14802 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 4, 2026 - REZ #25-13 (5058 Imperial Street) 1. 14802 Amend No. 4 2026 REZ 25-13 (5058 Imperial St).ACD 2026.pdf Purpose: to permit the construction of a 20-storey mixed-use multiple-family market rental residential development fronting Imperial Street with a double-height commercial storey at street level. The proposed development concept supersedes a previously approved plan for a six-storey market and non-market rental apartment building on the site approved through REZ #20-36 (Item 8.4, PDC Report, Council February 24, 2026) 14802 Amend No. 4 2026 REZ 25-13 (5058 Imperial St).ACD 2026.pdf

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Zoning Bylaw Amendment for 6800 Lougheed Hwy
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider a change to the zoning rules for 6800 Lougheed Highway. The proposal would amend the Burnaby Lake Village Conceptual Master Plan. This involves updating the site plan with new building heights and sizes. It also includes more open space. This change aligns with the city's updated Official Community Plan (OCP) 2050 and related zoning rules (R6 and CM1). A Master Plan (MP) is a detailed plan guiding future development.
briefs.action_required: Monitor City Council's decision, as it may impact future development in the area.
briefs.source_body: 11.1.2 #14803 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 5, 2026 - REZ #25-18 (6800 Lougheed Hwy) 1. 14803 Amendment No. 5 2026 REZ 25-18 (6800 Lougheed Hwy).ACD 2026.pdf Purpose: to advance a Master Plan (MP) amendment to the Burnaby Lake Village Conceptual Master Plan to establish a revised site plan with updated height and massing in alignment with the recently adopted OCP 2050 and associated height-based zoning districts (R6 and CM1), as well as expanded open space provisions for the site. No development or subdivision is proposed through the subject MP amendment (Item 8.5., PDC Report, Council February 24, 2026) 14803 Amendment No. 5 2026 REZ 25-18 (6800 Lougheed Hwy).ACD 2026.pdf

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Zoning Bylaw Amendment for High-Rise on Springer Ave
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider a change to the zoning rules for 2209 and 2225 Springer Avenue. The proposal would allow a developer to build a 49-story high-rise apartment building. The building would include a 9-story base with retail stores facing the street.
briefs.action_required: Monitor City Council meetings for updates on this rezoning application.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt
briefs.source_body: 11.1.3 #14804 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 6, 2026 - REZ #25-20 (2209 & 2225 Springer Avenue) 1. 14804 Amendment No. 6 2026 REZ 25-20 (2209 and 2255 Springer Ave).CD 2026.pdf Purpose: to permit the construction of a single high-rise apartment tower of up to 49 storeys inclusive of a 9 storey terraced podium with street fronting retail (Item 8.6., PDC Report, Council February 24, 2026) 14804 Amendment No. 6 2026 REZ 25-20 (2209 and 2255 Springer Ave).CD 2026.pdf

briefs.title: Council to consider: Changes to Burnaby parking and EV charging rules
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider changes to the city's parking meter and electric vehicle (EV) charging bylaw. A proposed bylaw amendment would update the rules for the public EV charging network. Council will vote on the proposed changes at an upcoming meeting.

briefs.title: Council to Vote: Fees for Public EV Charging Network
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider amending the city's Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw. The change would set fees for using Burnaby's public electric vehicle (EV) charging network. Council is scheduled to vote on the proposed bylaw at an upcoming meeting.

briefs.title: Council to vote on: Electric Vehicle Charging Meter Bylaw Amendment in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider amending the Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw. The change relates to public electric vehicle (EV) charging stations. This could affect how parking and charging are managed for EVs in the city.
briefs.action_required: Stay informed about the council's decision, as it may impact EV charging for your business.

briefs.title: Proposed: Rezoning Opposition at 5058 Imperial Street
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider a petition against rezoning at 5058 Imperial Street. The petition, submitted by residents of 5051 Imperial Street and nearby properties, expresses their opposition to Rezoning Application #25-13. Council will review the petition and supplementary materials to inform their decision on the rezoning proposal.
briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant, Construction & Development
